title: Correct remote lease expiry across clock skew
prompt: Production lease decisions disagree with the remote authority in both directions: one already-expired lease remains active while a valid lease from another producer is rejected. Read `incident/distributed-log.jsonl` and correlate each producer timestamp with the local consumer-received timestamp. Remote lease issue/expiry values are in the producer's wall-clock domain; `DistributedLogClockSkew`, built from those paired log timestamps, is already injected into `LeaseExpiryDecider`. Repair the remote-expiry decision so it compares instants in the same clock domain. Preserve the audit contract exactly: `RemoteIssuedAtUtc` and `RemoteExpiresAtUtc` remain the unmodified authority values, and `EvaluatedAtLocalUtc` remains the injected local wall time. Preserve the independent local-age safety limit as monotonic duration logic through `ISystemClock.GetElapsedTime`; do not derive elapsed time from wall-clock timestamps or apply skew to it. Keep the public API and decision precedence unchanged. Do not modify incident evidence or protected tests, add packages, read the machine clock directly, sleep, or use network access. Verification is offline and deterministic and succeeds with `python3 -B tests/verify.py` from the repository root.

full_distill.primary_skill: correlate paired producer and consumer log timestamps to translate an injected local wall instant into the remote clock domain without changing audit or monotonic time semantics
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the protected harness reproduces both incident directions: with a producer five minutes ahead the service keeps an authority-expired lease active, and with a producer five minutes behind it rejects a lease the authority still considers active
full_distill.success_contract: remote expiry uses the distributed-log-derived injected skew; authority and local audit timestamps remain unmodified; local maximum age still uses injected monotonic elapsed time; the established remote-deadline decision precedence remains intact
adversarial_factors: ahead and behind producers pin the skew sign and reject a one-direction special case
an exact translated deadline pins the inclusive expiry boundary
audit assertions reject rewriting remote lease timestamps or reporting skew-adjusted local evaluation time
a local wall-clock jump paired with monotonic advancement rejects wall-time subtraction for duration logic
a case where remote expiry and maximum local age both elapsed preserves the existing remote-deadline precedence
